name: Autonomous Administration of North and East Syria (AANES / Rojava / ڕۆژاوا)
summary: The Autonomous Administration of North and East Syria (AANES — formally Rojava ڕۆژاوا 'West' in Kurdish, also Northern Syria Democratic Federation) is a Northeastern Syrian mixed civil-law/customary-law de facto autonomous region of Syria — structurally distinctive globally as the only sub-state-level Kurdish jurisdiction operating formal Democratic Confederalism framework (Apo Öcalan-Bookchin Democratic Confederalism framework — distinct council-democracy-libertarian-municipalism political-organisational framework, distinct from rest-of-Kurdish-jurisdiction frameworks in Iraq, Turkey, Iran which operate conventional nation-state autonomy frameworks), as the only modern Syrian sub-national territory established via post-2011 Syrian Civil War framework with formal multi-ethnic Kurdish-Arab-Syriac-Christian-Turkmen self-governance framework (2014 Charter of the Social Contract of the Cantons + 2016 Federation of Northern Syria + 2018 Constitution of the Federation of Northern Syria-Rojava — distinct from Iraqi Kurdistan's mono-ethnic Kurdish framework), as the central jurisdiction of the most militarily distinctive modern Kurdish self-governance jurisdiction (Syrian Democratic Forces SDF + People's Defence Units YPG + Women's Defence Units YPJ — central role in 2014-2019 international anti-ISIS coalition framework with US-led international military support framework), and as the only modern de facto autonomous region operating formal gender-equality co-governance framework (formal requirement for male+female co-chair leadership at all administrative levels — Jineolojî 'science of women' Kurdish-feminist theoretical framework, distinct from rest-of-Kurdish-jurisdiction frameworks). Rojava historically operated under Ottoman vilayet framework + French Mandate Syria 1920-1946 + Syrian Republic frameworks 1946-2012, achieved de facto autonomy 19 July 2012 following Syrian Civil War 2011-onwards withdrawal of Syrian Government forces (Rojava Revolution framework), established the Federation of Northern Syria 2016, ratified the Constitution of the Federation of Northern Syria-Rojava 2018, and continues to operate as de facto autonomous region without formal constitutional recognition by Syrian Government. AANES comprises ~50,000 km² with a population of ~2 million across Cizîrê + Euphrates + Afrin + Manbij + Tabqa + Raqqa + Deir ez-Zor cantons. Family-law framework operates under a dual customary-Kurdish framework + AANES Family-Law Code framework + residual Syrian-Civil-Code framework. The People's Defence Court is the primary court framework; final appellate jurisdiction is contested between AANES Appellate Court + Syrian Federal Court. AANES is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label. Syria is a Hague Convention 1980 signatory but not a party — AANES is governed by Syrian non-ratification status + de facto AANES Family-Law Code framework.

statutory_framework.citation: Constitution of the Federation of Northern Syria-Rojava 2018
statutory_framework.title: Constitution of the Federation of Northern Syria-Rojava
statutory_framework.relevance: AANES Constitution ratified 2018 — formal multi-ethnic Kurdish-Arab-Syriac-Christian-Turkmen self-governance framework with formal gender-equality co-governance framework.

statutory_framework.citation: Charter of the Social Contract of the Cantons 2014
statutory_framework.title: Charter of the Social Contract
statutory_framework.relevance: AANES Charter of the Social Contract of 9 January 2014 — original constitutional framework establishing Cizîrê + Euphrates + Afrin cantons.

statutory_framework.title: AANES Family-Law Code
statutory_framework.relevance: AANES Family-Law Code framework 2014+ — formal banning of underage marriage, polygamy, honour killings + formal gender-equality framework, distinct from residual Syrian-Civil-Code framework.

statutory_framework.title: Democratic Confederalism Framework
statutory_framework.relevance: Apo Öcalan-Bookchin Democratic Confederalism framework — distinct council-democracy-libertarian-municipalism political-organisational framework underlying AANES governance.

apex_courts.name: AANES Appellate Court
apex_courts.role: Apex regional appellate court for civil and criminal matters from AANES.

apex_courts.name: Syrian Federal Court (contested jurisdiction)
apex_courts.role: Syrian Federal Court — contested jurisdiction over AANES matters.

professional_regulators.name: AANES Health Authority
professional_regulators.role: AANES regional professional regulatory framework applicable in AANES.

anonymisation_convention: AANES family-court decisions are anonymised per AANES + Syrian court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Syrian Civil War begins
key_developments.description: Syrian Civil War 15 March 2011-onwards — Arab Spring framework catalysing AANES de facto autonomy establishment.

key_developments.title: Rojava Revolution + de facto autonomy
key_developments.description: Rojava Revolution 19 July 2012 — withdrawal of Syrian Government forces from Northeastern Syria + establishment of de facto AANES autonomy.

key_developments.title: Charter of the Social Contract
key_developments.description: AANES Charter of the Social Contract of the Cantons ratified 9 January 2014 — original constitutional framework.

key_developments.title: Siege of Kobani + anti-ISIS coalition
key_developments.description: Siege of Kobani 13 September 2014 - 26 January 2015 — pivotal anti-ISIS battle framework + beginning of US-led international anti-ISIS coalition framework with AANES SDF/YPG/YPJ central role.

key_developments.title: Federation of Northern Syria established
key_developments.description: Federation of Northern Syria established 17 March 2016 — formal federalist constitutional framework.

key_developments.title: Constitution of the Federation of Northern Syria-Rojava
key_developments.description: AANES Constitution of the Federation of Northern Syria-Rojava ratified 2018.

key_developments.title: Defeat of ISIS Caliphate + Baghuz battle
key_developments.description: Defeat of ISIS Caliphate 23 March 2019 via Battle of Baghuz Fawqani — culmination of 5-year AANES SDF/YPG/YPJ central role in international anti-ISIS coalition framework.

key_developments.title: Turkish offensive on AANES
key_developments.description: Turkish offensive on AANES Operation Peace Spring 9-23 October 2019 — formal Turkish military operation against AANES.

key_developments.title: Fall of Assad regime
key_developments.description: Fall of Bashar al-Assad regime December 2024 — transition to post-Assad Syrian framework with AANES status under reconsideration.

structural_findings: AANES operates a mixed de facto Syrian civil-law/customary-Kurdish + AANES-specific de facto autonomous region framework — places AANES in the Northeastern Syrian de facto autonomous-region cluster.
Only sub-state-level Kurdish jurisdiction operating formal Democratic Confederalism framework is structurally distinctive globally — Apo Öcalan-Bookchin Democratic Confederalism framework.

Only modern Syrian sub-national territory established via post-2011 Syrian Civil War framework with formal multi-ethnic self-governance framework is structurally distinctive globally — distinct from Iraqi Kurdistan's mono-ethnic Kurdish framework.

Central jurisdiction of most militarily distinctive modern Kurdish self-governance jurisdiction is structurally distinctive globally — central role in 2014-2019 international anti-ISIS coalition framework.

Only modern de facto autonomous region operating formal gender-equality co-governance framework is structurally distinctive globally — formal male+female co-chair leadership requirement + Jineolojî Kurdish-feminist theoretical framework.

Only sub-state-level Kurdish jurisdiction operating without formal constitutional recognition is structurally distinctive globally — distinct from Iraqi Kurdistan's 2005 Iraqi Constitution Article 117 formal constitutional recognition.

AANES SDF/YPG/YPJ + US-led international anti-ISIS coalition military framework is structurally distinctive globally — only sub-state-level entity with formal multi-year US-led international military support framework.
editorial_notes: AANES jurisdiction sidecar — mixed de facto Syrian civil-law/customary-Kurdish Northeastern Syrian de facto autonomous region (Constitution of the Federation of Northern Syria-Rojava 2018 + Charter of the Social Contract 2014 + AANES Family-Law Code 2014+ + Democratic Confederalism Framework Öcalan-Bookchin + Rojava Revolution 2012 + Siege of Kobani 2014-2015 + Federation of Northern Syria 2016 + Defeat of ISIS Caliphate 2019 + Turkish Operation Peace Spring 2019 + Fall of Assad 2024 + Syrian non-Hague-1980). Only sub-state-level Kurdish jurisdiction operating formal Democratic Confederalism framework globally + only modern Syrian sub-national territory with formal multi-ethnic Kurdish-Arab-Syriac-Christian-Turkmen self-governance framework + central jurisdiction of most militarily distinctive modern Kurdish self-governance jurisdiction + only modern de facto autonomous region operating formal gender-equality co-governance framework + only sub-state-level Kurdish jurisdiction operating without formal constitutional recognition.
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.

Joins Northeastern-Syrian + mixed Syrian-civil-law/customary-Kurdish + Syrian-de-facto-autonomous-region cluster + Democratic-Confederalism-Öcalan-Bookchin-globally-distinctive + multi-ethnic-Kurdish-Arab-Syriac-Christian-Turkmen-framework + gender-equality-co-governance-Jineolojî + 2014-2019-international-anti-ISIS-coalition-SDF-YPG-YPJ + Rojava-Revolution-2012 + Charter-of-the-Social-Contract-2014 + Constitution-Federation-of-Northern-Syria-Rojava-2018 + Fall-of-Assad-2024 + Syrian-non-Hague-1980 clusters within the corpus.
